steps.m: Individual letters: Alif, Lam, Meem, Ra
steps.n: These are the names of the individual Arabic letters that make up this combination

steps.m: Alif Lam Meem Ra (special Quranic opening letters)
steps.n: These special letters (huroof muqatta'at) appear at the start of some Quranic chapters. While we know how to read them, their deeper meaning is considered a divine mystery that only Allah knows.
This is the basic feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to something. The ت with kasra (تِ) shows it's talking about a feminine thing.
Adding ل with sukun (لْ) changes it from pointing to something close to pointing to something farther away

steps.m: that (feminine, farther away, pointing to ءَايٰتُ/verses)
steps.n: Adding ك with fatha (كَ) completes the word. In this ayah, it's pointing to the verses (ءَايٰتُ) that are about to be mentioned.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a feminine noun meaning 'sign' or 'verse'. The first letter changed from أ to ء with a long آ after it because of special spelling rules.
Makes the word plural and adds ُ to show this is the subject being pointed to by تِلْكَ (those)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

The pattern creates a noun meaning the result of writing - a book. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after آيات in a possession relationship, where آيات (signs/verses) possesses الكتاب (the book).
Added ٱلْ to make it definite ('the book') and ِ to show it's possessed by آيات (verses/signs)
This is the basic relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to things, like saying 'the book that I read'
Adding ال with shadda makes it more specific, pointing to a particular person or thing being described

steps.m: and that which (connects to تِلْكَ ءَايٰتُ ٱلْكِتٰبِ)
steps.n: The و connects this to the previous phrase about 'these are the verses of the Book'. The ٓ shows where to pause when reading.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show someone is causing the action to happen
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than someone doing it
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added كَ at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This word connects to أُنزِلَ (was sent down) to show what was sent down to.
This is a preposition showing that the revelation came from your Lord. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger and turns it into a noun meaning 'one who does this a lot'
Adding kasra (i) sound at the end shows this thing belongs to someone, like saying 'my Lord'
Adding كَ shows the Lord belongs to you. This whole phrase comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.
The doubled letter shows this is the essential quality or concept of truth itself
Adding ال makes it specific, and the ُّ shows this word is the subject of أُنزِلَ (was sent down) in this ayah

Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before. This word contrasts with ٱلْحَقُّ (the truth) from earlier in the ayah - it's saying 'it is the truth, BUT most people don't believe'.
The word means 'but' with 'and' at the start, and the shadda makes it stronger to mean 'but indeed'. It shows a clear opposite to the previous word ٱلْحَقُّ.
Added vowels to make it mean 'it became many' in the past
Added أَ at the beginning to change from 'became many' to 'most/majority' - like saying 'more than others'. Shows that most people (from ٱلنَّاسِ) don't believe.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: The root means 'to be social' and adding alif makes it mean 'people who are social with each other'. Like how humans live together in groups.
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter with shadda
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the people' instead of just 'people'

steps.m: of the people (possessed by أَكْثَرَ - most)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or is connected to another word (أَكْثَرَ - most), forming 'most of the people'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) into 'they do not believe'. It's one of the most common words used to say 'no' or 'not' in Arabic.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Root أمن became آمَنَ by adding a long alif at the start, changing it from 'to trust' into 'he trusted/believed' - like changing from an idea into an action
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza changed form due to pronunciation rules.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
The suffix shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'

Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال. The ا and ل merge in a unique way to make Allah's special name. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action in this sentence.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to what they're describing
Adding ال makes it definite and adding shadda emphasizes the connection. This word connects Allah (اللَّهُ) to what He did - raising the heavens.

steps.m: to lift up or raise something higher

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is describing what Allah did with the heavens (السَّمٰوٰتِ) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a word meaning 'to rise up' into the noun 'sky' by adding ء at the end - this makes sense since the sky is high up above us
Made the word plural by adding وات at the end
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: of the heavens/skies (object of رَفَعَ - raised)
steps.n: Changed to show it's being acted upon (by adding -i at end), with a different way of writing the long 'aa' sound in middle

steps.m: to change or be different from something

steps.n: The root letters were given specific vowels (َ on غ and ْ on ي) to make a noun meaning 'other than' instead of the verb 'to change'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/without'. This word describes عَمَدٍ (pillars) in the ayah.

steps.m: to prop up or support something

steps.n: Adding fathas (َ) to each letter turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'he propped up'
The word changed from a verb (he supported) to a plural noun (pillars) that is being described as missing/without. The ٍ ending shows it's connected to the word بِغَيْرِ (without)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added ت at the beginning to make it 'you' instead of 'he', and the middle letter changed to make it easier to say
Added ون to change from 'you (singular) see' to 'you all see' - like changing from 'you look' to 'you all look'
Added هَا which refers back to the pillars (عَمَدٍ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. Here it connects Allah raising the heavens (رَفَعَ) with Him establishing Himself (ٱسْتَوَىٰ) on the throne.

steps.m: to be straight, level, or settled

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a-a
Added 'ist' at start to show doing action to/for oneself (like settling oneself)
Changed the ending to ى which is common for past tense verbs ending in weak letters
Added hamzat al-wasl (ٱ) which helps pronunciation at the start of words
This is a preposition that shows Allah is established upon His throne in a way that befits His majesty. It connects عَرْشِ (throne) to the verb اسْتَوَىٰ (He rose/established)

steps.m: to raise up, elevate, build something high

steps.n: The root letters ع-ر-ش meaning 'to raise up' became a physical object عَرْش meaning 'throne' by using a simple noun pattern. Like how a chair is something raised up to sit on.
The word becomes definite with 'the' and shows possession with the kasra

steps.m: to control or make something serve a purpose

steps.n: The doubled خ shows that Allah is exercising complete control over these powerful celestial bodies. This word connects to الشَّمْسَ (the sun) and الْقَمَرَ (the moon) which come right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: and He controlled/subjected (the sun and moon)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this action with the previous one (رَفَعَ - He raised) in the ayah, showing these are both examples of Allah's power
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to make the noun for the thing that shines brightly
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the sun' instead of just any sun. The ش gets a shadda because it comes after ٱل.

steps.m: the sun (object of وَسَخَّرَ - and He subjected)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show that the sun is being acted upon - it's the object that Allah subjected (along with the moon) in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun for the celestial body
Added ال to specify this particular moon and added fatha at the end because it's the object of وَسَخَّرَ (and He subjected/made subservient)

steps.m: and the moon (object of وَسَخَّرَ - He subjected)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect it with الشَّمْسَ (the sun) as things that Allah has subjected
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete

The double damma (ٌ) shows this word is the subject doing the action in يَجْرِى (runs/flows). In this ayah, it refers to both the sun and moon mentioned before, showing they each flow for an appointed term.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.m: it flows/runs (subject of كُلٌّ - each one)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle letter gets sukun (ْ) and the last root letter gets kasra (ِ). The ي at the end is written as ى because it's at the end of the word.
Connected the letters with fatha vowels to create a noun meaning a specific point in time

steps.m: for an appointed time (relates to يَجْرِى - runs/moves)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose ('for') and tanwin kasra at end to show it's indefinite. This word explains why everything moves - it's for an appointed time.
The root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he', and the last letter و changed to ا because it's at the end
Added emphasis by doubling the middle letter. The ا changes to ى at the end.
Added مُ at the beginning to show this is something that receives the action of naming
Adding shadda on م makes the sound stronger to emphasize this is something that receives the action
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra care and thoroughness
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word refers to Allah's continuous management of affairs.
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into the noun 'matter/affair' by adding specific vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon). This is the thing being managed in the phrase.

steps.m: the matter (being managed by يُدَبِّرُ - He manages)
steps.n: The ال makes it specific - this isn't just any matter, but the particular matter being discussed

steps.m: to separate or divide into distinct parts

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning more intense - changing from simple separation to detailed explanation

steps.m: he explains in detail (subject of يُدَبِّرُ - he arranges)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word describes what Allah is doing now, connected to the previous verb يُدَبِّرُ (he arranges).

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: The root letters combine with a long 'aa' sound and 'ah' ending to make a noun meaning 'sign'
Added ات to make it plural, showing there are many signs
Added ال to show these are specific signs being talked about

steps.m: the signs or miracles (object of يُفَصِّلُ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is the object of يُفَصِّلُ (he explains in detail)
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation

steps.m: so that you all (might) (connected to تُوقِنُونَ - you all are certain)
steps.n: Added كُم to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. In this ayah, it connects to تُوقِنُونَ (you all are certain) to express the hope that they will become certain.
Changed the verb into a noun that means the act of meeting
Added بِ at the start to show how the meeting relates to being certain (تُوقِنُونَ)
Added ِ at the end to connect it to the next word رَبِّكُمْ (your Lord)

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and develop something to its full potential
Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely and perfectly
The kasra shows this word is connected to something coming after it
Added كُمْ which means 'your' when talking to many people
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding و and changing first vowel to ُ (u)
Changed initial ي to ت to make second person singular present tense

steps.m: you all become completely sure (about meeting your Lord)
steps.n: Added plural suffix ون to make second person masculine plural
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the pronoun
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This pronoun 'he' is doing the action in مَدَّ (spread out) that comes after it.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect clauses by pointing to something or someone

steps.m: the one who (connects وَهُوَ to what follows)
steps.n: Adding ال with shadda makes it definite and emphatic. This word connects the previous phrase 'and He' (وَهُوَ) to the following description about spreading out the earth.
Added fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb showing one person did the action
Combined the doubled middle letter with shadda (ّ) to show this is done thoroughly and completely. This word connects to الْأَرْضَ (the earth) which comes right after it.
Basic noun form with fatha and sukoon marks added to root letters

steps.m: the earth (object of مَدَّ - spread out)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite ('the earth') and fatḥa at the end shows it receives the action of being spread out in this ayah

steps.m: to make or put something in a certain way
he made/put (something that is mentioned in فِيهَا رَوٰسِىَ وَأَنْهٰرًا)
steps.n: Added fathas to show this happened in the past. This word connects to what comes after it - the mountains and rivers that were made/put.

steps.m: and he made/put (something that is mentioned in فِيهَا رَوٰسِىَ وَأَنْهٰرًا)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the verse
Basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'

steps.m: in it (feminine, referring to الْأَرْضَ/the earth)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it', referring back to 'the earth' (الْأَرْضَ) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: to be firmly fixed or anchored

steps.n: Changes the concept of being fixed into something that does the fixing

steps.m: mountains (as firmly fixed anchors) (object of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Changes from single 'anchor' to plural 'anchors' by adding وَ, used specially for mountains that keep the earth stable

steps.m: mountains (as firmly fixed anchors) (object of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Changes the spelling to use a long alif (ٰ) and final alif (ى) - this is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'river'
Changed to plural form meaning multiple rivers
Added ًا to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence (the thing being placed)
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is one of the objects of جَعَلَ (He placed/made)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' that connects to the phrase 'all fruits' that follows it

steps.m: and from (connects to كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرٰتِ - 'all fruits')
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. The full meaning is 'and from all fruits'
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything in the group being discussed. In this ayah, it's talking about all types of fruits.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to الثَّمَرٰتِ (the fruits) that comes right after it
Added a double mark (shadda) to make the word mean 'lots of fruit' or 'abundant fruit'
Added feminine plural ending ات to show multiple fruits
Added ال to make it specific/definite fruits
Added kasra at end to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in the phrase مِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرٰتِ (from all fruits)

steps.m: to put/place/make something into a particular state
he made/placed (connected to رَوٰسِىَ mountains and أَنْهٰرًا rivers)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is telling us He placed mountains and rivers on the earth.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. It doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to الْأَرْضَ - the earth)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'the earth' mentioned earlier in the verse. This shows what is being described as containing things.

steps.m: to pair up or make into pairs

steps.n: Root letters get vowels to make the noun meaning 'pair/spouse'

steps.m: two pairs (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added the dual ending يْنِ to show exactly two of something. This word is what Allah made (the object of جَعَلَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added ٱ at start, changed vowels, and added يْنِ at end to make it mean 'two'. This word describes زَوْجَيْنِ (a pair) in the ayah.

steps.m: to cover or envelop something completely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now). The middle letter got a kasra (ِ) to show it's being done to something. The ending changed to ي.
Changed the ending to ى which is common for this type of verb
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: the night (object being covered by يُغْشِى)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and َ at the end to show it's the object being covered. This word is being acted upon by يُغْشِى (he covers) in this ayah.
Root letters joined with vowels to make the word for 'river' - something that flows brightly
Added alif to create noun for the time when brightness flows
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ن as a result
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُغْشِى (covers)
Added kasra under إ to show correct pronunciation. This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it.
Added shadda to make the ن sound stronger, which makes the meaning stronger too - like saying 'truly' instead of just 'indeed'
This is a basic preposition showing where something is located. In this ayah, it shows where the mountains (رَوٰسِىَ) and rivers (أَنْهٰرًا) are placed.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun ذا which means 'this'
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ذ for proper pronunciation
Added ل with kasra (ِ) to change the meaning from 'this' to 'that' by adding distance
Added ك with fatha (َ) at the end to show greater distance

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة makes this a feminine noun meaning 'sign'
Added ات to make it plural, showing multiple signs
Added two dots below (ٍ) to show this word is the object - the thing receiving the action
Added لَ at start to emphasize the importance of these signs

steps.m: people who stand together (subject of يَتَفَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks. The root meaning of standing together developed into meaning a group of people who unite for a purpose. This word is the subject of يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (they reflect/think deeply).
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is indefinite - meaning any group of people, not a specific one

steps.m: for a people who stand together (subject of يَتَفَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Added لِ with shadda to mean 'for/to'. The shadda makes the meaning stronger.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ (ta) at start and doubled middle letter to show doing the action to oneself with extra effort
Changes from past tense (he reflected) to present tense (he reflects) by adding يَ at the start
Added plural suffix ون to indicate multiple people thinking
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
The و connects this with what came before, showing these statements are related

steps.m: and in (connected to الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. This preposition introduces what comes next (الْأَرْضِ - the earth)
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any earth. The ٱ is a special form of ا used at the start of words.
The ِ at the end shows this word comes after the word في (in) in the ayah. In Arabic, words after في get this ِ ending.

steps.m: to cut or separate into pieces

steps.n: Changes the verb into a plural noun showing the results of cutting - the pieces themselves

steps.m: pieces (as the subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The double damma shows this word is the subject in this part of the verse, referring to the pieces/tracts of land mentioned

steps.m: to be near or next to something

steps.n: Root meaning 'to be near' becomes active participle meaning 'neighboring'
Adding مُ turns the word into a noun meaning 'one who does the action'

steps.m: multiple things next to each other
steps.n: Added ات to show we're talking about multiple things

steps.m: multiple things next to each other (with emphasis)
steps.n: The doubled م adds extra emphasis to show these things are definitely next to each other
Added vowels and connected the letters together. The doubled ن shows extra strength in the meaning.

steps.m: a garden (something that covers with its shade)
steps.n: The ة changes it from an action to a place or thing that does that action. A garden 'covers' with its shade and plants.
Makes the word plural. This word is in the nominative case as it is part of a list of subjects being described in the ayah.
Adds 'and' to the beginning and shows it's indefinite and in subject position
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is made of something

steps.m: from/of (after a word ending in ن or م)
steps.n: Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م, which combines with the م of مِن. This is just a pronunciation change.

steps.m: from/of (after a word ending in ن or م)
steps.n: Added sukun to show the ن has no vowel. This helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to grow grapes, to produce grapes

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning grapes
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter
Added genitive case ending because it comes after the preposition مِنْ (from/of). The alif is written as ٰ when followed by a case ending.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and change it from an action to a thing. This shows what is grown rather than the act of growing.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to the previous items in the list being described in the verse
Added tanween (double damma) at the end to make the word indefinite
The ي in the middle shows there are lots of palm trees
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to the previous items being listed in the verse (gardens of grapes and crops)

steps.m: to grow together or be similar (like palm trees from one root)
one of a pair (especially of palm trees growing from same root)
steps.n: Root letters ص-ن-و got short 'i' and sukoon marks to make a noun meaning 'one of a pair'

steps.m: pairs (of palm trees growing from same root)
steps.n: Forms the dual/plural noun meaning 'pairs' - specifically referring to palm trees growing from the same root in this ayah

steps.m: pairs (of palm trees growing from same root) in general
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to show this is talking about pairs of trees in general, not specific ones

steps.m: to be different, to change into something else

steps.n: Changed from a verb root meaning 'to change' into a noun meaning 'other than' by adding vowel marks. The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is doing something in the sentence
Added 'and' at beginning and damma for nominative case

steps.m: to grow together or in pairs
one of a pair (especially of date palms growing from same root)
steps.n: Root letters got vowel marks (ِ and ْ) to make a word meaning 'one of a pair'
Added ان to show it's talking about two things that are paired

steps.m: pairs (of date palms growing from same root)
steps.n: Added two dots under the last letter to show we're talking about multiple pairs of palm trees growing together, not just one pair
Root letters س-ق-ي became a past tense verb by adding fathas. The ي at the end becomes ى when it has a fatha.
Added يُ at start to show passive voice (something being done to it). Added sukun after first root letter.

steps.m: it is given drink (object of بِمَآءٍ وٰحِدٍ)
steps.n: Added alif maksura mark to show this is the end of the word. This word is what is being watered with one water (بِمَآءٍ وٰحِدٍ) in this ayah.
The root letters transform into the common word for water
Added tanween kasra to show this word is being acted upon by something else in the sentence

steps.m: with water (used to water يُسْقَىٰ plants)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is the water used to water the plants mentioned in the verse
Root meaning 'one' becomes a word meaning 'single/one' by adding vowels

Added the ٍ mark at the end to show this word belongs to or describes something else. Also, the long alif (ا) changed to a small alif above (ٰ) which is just a different way of writing the same sound.
Adding the shadda makes the meaning active - someone is causing something to be superior
Changed from 'he' to 'we' and past to present tense by adding نُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and we make superior (some over others in taste)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect with previous phrase. This word describes how Allah makes some fruits and plants superior to others in taste.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb نُفَضِّلُ (We prefer)
Added ها which means 'it' referring back to the previously mentioned gardens and crops

This is a preposition that shows one thing is positioned above or has superiority over another. In this ayah, it connects 'some of them' (بَعْضَهَا) with 'others' (بَعْضٍ) to show how some parts are preferred over others.
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make the root word mean 'some' or 'a part'
Added two dots under the last letter (ٍ) to show this word connects to another word that owns or possesses it
This is a preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the different pieces of land are located in/within the earth.
The root أكل means 'to eat', but when voweled as أُكُل it becomes a plural noun meaning 'types of food' - like changing 'eat' to 'foods'

steps.m: of the fruit/produce (in relation to taste/quality)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to another word that owns or describes it

steps.m: the fruit/produce (that is being compared in taste/quality)
steps.n: Adding 'al' (ال) makes it 'the specific' fruit/produce being discussed
Starting with the basic particle that shows emphasis. The kasra (ِ) under the إ is added to help with pronunciation.
The shadda (ّ) doubles the ن sound to make the emphasis stronger, like saying 'truly' instead of just 'indeed'
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it specifically refers to differences in the fruits/produce mentioned.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is written as a small mark above the ذ to show it's a special word
Adding لِ changes the word from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the signs in the previous ayah)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word and makes it point to something specific that was mentioned before

steps.m: to show, indicate, or be a sign

steps.n: Root letters combine to make 'sign', with first letter (hamza) getting a fatha and middle letter (yaa) becoming a long 'aa' sound. The final yaa is dropped.
Added feminine plural ending ات to show multiple signs
Added tanween (double kasra) to show indefiniteness

steps.m: surely/indeed signs (emphasized signs in إِنَّ فِى ذٰلِكَ)
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to emphasize the signs being discussed. This word is part of إِنَّ فِى ذٰلِكَ لَءَايٰتٍ (indeed in that are signs)
The root meaning of standing/rising is transformed into a collective noun for people who stand together
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'people who stand together as one'
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' in English) and is being acted upon by the preposition
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' a people
Adding shadda (doubled L sound) makes the meaning stronger - 'surely/definitely for'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and vowel pattern change
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + إن (if)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show past tense

steps.m: you (are) amazed (in response to إِن if)
steps.n: The ت at the start shows it's talking about 'you'. The sukun marks (ْ) show this is a command or conditional form, matching with إِن (if) before it.
Root becomes a simple noun meaning 'a feeling of wonder/amazement'

steps.m: then [it is] a wonder! (connected to تَعْجَبْ - you wonder)
steps.n: The فَ (fa) means 'then/so', connecting it to someone wondering before. The word became a complete exclamation like saying 'What a wonder!'
Changed from basic root 'to say' into 'saying' by adding vowels, where ُ shows this is the main doer
Added هُمْ to show this saying belongs to them
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that refers to a future time
The initial إ changes to ء to make it easier to pronounce with the coming question particle
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question expressing surprise or disbelief. In this ayah, it's used by disbelievers questioning the possibility of resurrection.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Changes singular 'soil' to plural by adding vowel marks
Adds alif to show this is the substance/material of dust/earth
Changed from regular dust/soil to dust as a direct object (something being acted upon)
Added نا at the end to show this refers to 'we'

steps.m: Is it true that we...? (expressing disbelief)
steps.n: Added أَءِ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question expressing disbelief or amazement. This is a special Quranic questioning form that shows the speakers are doubting or rejecting what they're asking about.
Basic preposition showing location or presence within something
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like adding 'surely' or 'indeed' in English

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun that means the act or concept of creating

steps.m: a creation (being acted upon in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this word is receiving the action and is indefinite (not specific). In this ayah, it's describing what kind of thing they will be in - a new creation.

steps.m: to make something new or to renew

steps.n: Added ي between the doubled letters and vowels to make an adjective meaning 'new'
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this describes the word خَلْقٍ (creation) in the phrase 'in a new creation'
Basic demonstrative form for pointing to things
Adding ئِ shows distance - points to things far away
Adding كَ adds emphasis and makes it more specific
Adding the elongation marks (ٰٓ) and small و (۟) helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ٱلَّ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ينَ makes it refer to multiple people. This word connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) which comes right after it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense singular form
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together
The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely
Added بِ at the beginning to show connection or relationship with the master/nurturer (disbelief in their relationship with their nurturer is being discussed in this ayah)

steps.m: by/with their master/nurturer (referring to those who disbelieve)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show it's their master/nurturer. In this ayah, it refers to those who disbelieve rejecting their relationship with Allah who nurtures them.
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are far away, like saying 'those people over there' in English

steps.m: and those (pointing to the disbelievers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. In this ayah, it's specifically pointing back to the disbelievers who were just mentioned.

steps.m: to bind or restrain with chains

steps.n: The root غ-ل-ل about binding becomes أَغْلَال (aghlāl) which means multiple chains or shackles. Like how one chain is غُلّ (ghull), multiple chains are أَغْلَال (aghlāl)
Added ٱلْ (the) to show these are specific chains, and ُ at the end to show this is the subject of a sentence (these chains are around their necks)
Basic preposition showing location or being part of something. In this ayah, it connects 'we were' (كُنَّا) with 'creation' (خَلْقٍ) to say 'we were in a creation'.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Changed the word to show many necks instead of just one by adding أ at the start and ا after ن

steps.m: their necks (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier as ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show these necks belong to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to things or people that are far away. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.
Added damma (ُ) on the first letter to show proper pronunciation
Added وَ at the beginning which means 'and', connecting this word to what came before it

steps.m: and those (referring to the disbelievers mentioned)
steps.n: Changed the ending to the standard Quranic spelling with كَ at the end. In this ayah, it refers back to 'those who disbelieved in their Lord' (ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ)
Added alif after first letter to show who does the action
Added 'a' at the start and changed the middle to make it mean 'many companions' instead of just one

steps.m: companions (plural, subject of هُمْ فِيهَا خٰلِدُونَ)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject. The alif is written as ٰ which is just a writing style variation.
Changed the root into the noun 'fire' by adding fatha after first letter and moving the و to an alif
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and shadda on ن because it comes after ال

steps.m: they/them (referring to those who disbelieved in بِرَبِّهِمْ)
steps.n: This is a pronoun referring back to 'those who disbelieved in their Lord' in this ayah. The damma (ُ) shows it's the subject, while the sukun (ْ) marks the end of the word.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to النَّارِ - the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها at the end which refers back to النَّارِ (the Fire) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The Fire is feminine in Arabic, so we use the feminine pronoun ها.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to describe someone who does this action (like changing 'teach' to 'teacher')
Added ending to show this refers to many people (like adding 's' in English)

1. Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) which is a writing variation
2. Added َ at the end to show this describes the pronoun 'they' (هُمْ) in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ست to show seeking or requesting the action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added ك to show who they are trying to hurry

steps.m: and they seek to hurry you
steps.n: Added و at start to connect with previous phrase
Changed the root into a noun that means the evil thing itself
Added ي and doubled it to make it an adjective meaning 'very evil' (like turning 'bad' into 'terrible')
Added ة to make it refer to one specific evil action
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: with the evil deed (what they are hastening for in يَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show 'with' and kasra at end to connect with following words. This word shows what they are rushing for in the phrase 'they seek to hasten you'.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Changes from a verb meaning to a noun meaning 'before'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connected to الحسنة (the good) that comes after it, making the phrase 'before the good'
Added basic vowel pattern to make it an adjective
Adding ة makes it feminine and specifies one instance of goodness
Adding ال makes it definite. This word is what they are hastening for in the verse.

steps.m: of the good deed (object of يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ with قَبْلَ)
steps.n: Adding kasra shows this comes after قَبْلَ (before) in the verse
This is a particle that emphasizes the completed action خَلَتْ (have passed) that follows it
Added 'and' at beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about their hastening evil before good
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it past tense. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: it/she passed away (referring to examples/punishments مَثُلَات)
steps.n: Added ت to make it feminine because it refers to مَثُلَات (examples/punishments) mentioned later in the ayah. The final ا is dropped when ت is added.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلِ (before) with هِم (them), making the meaning 'before them'.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make it a noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِم ('them') at the end to show 'before them'. The extra مُ connects it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمَثُلٰتُ

steps.m: to represent something similar or serve as an example

steps.n: Changed into a plural noun showing specific instances. This word refers to previous examples of punishment that happened to people, and is the subject (doer) of خَلَتْ (passed) in this ayah.
Added 'Al' (the) at the start and 'u' ending to show it's the subject
This is an emphatic particle that adds emphasis to the following statement about your Lord (رَبَّكَ)
Added و at beginning to connect this statement with what came before it

steps.m: the one who nurtures completely (acted upon by إِنَّ)
steps.n: The root ر-ب-ب became رَبَّ (Lord/Master) with a fatha at the end because إِنَّ came before it and makes the word after it take fatha
Adding ك at the end shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to you. The word is part of إِنَّ رَبَّكَ (indeed, your Lord) in the ayah.
This is a special noun used to show possession. In this ayah, it connects to the word مَغْفِرَةٍ (forgiveness) to mean 'possessor of forgiveness'
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely or surely true. This emphasis connects to إِنَّ earlier in the ayah to make the statement even stronger.

steps.m: to forgive or cover up something

steps.n: Added مَ at start and ِ in middle to turn the action into a noun meaning the result of forgiveness
Added ة at the end to show one specific instance of forgiveness
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)

steps.m: to be friendly or familiar with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a common noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the people' instead of just 'people'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to another word
Added لِ with shadda to mean 'for/to' with emphasis
Adding shadda to emphasize the middle letter
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something is on top of or responsible for something else

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing responsibility for ظُلْمِهِمْ - their wrongdoing)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the end of the word. In this ayah, it connects to the word ظُلْمِهِمْ (their wrongdoing) to show they are responsible for their actions.
Changed the action of doing wrong into a noun meaning the wrong action itself, like changing 'wrong someone' to 'wrongdoing'
Added هِمْ at the end to show this wrongdoing belongs to them. This word is connected to عَلَىٰ (upon) in the ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. Here it emphasizes 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ).
The و connects this statement with the previous one, showing that both Allah's forgiveness and punishment are true
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together

steps.m: one who completely cares for and develops others
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Adds 'your' to show who the Lord belongs to. This word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which makes it an object.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word to show a strong quality. This describes العِقَابِ (punishment) in this ayah.
Added ُ to show this is the subject part of the phrase 'intense in punishment'
Added لَ at the beginning to emphasize how intense the punishment is
Changes verb into noun meaning the consequence/punishment that follows an action
Makes the punishment specific and shows it's what is being described as severe
Adds -i sound at end to show 'of' meaning
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
The ي at the start shows it's happening right now
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what comes before it
This is a special demonstrative pronoun used to refer to a group and connect them to what follows
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense. This word describes the action of those who are speaking (يَقُولُ) in this ayah.
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action instead of just one person
Added ۟ mark to show this is a plural form
This is a conditional particle that expresses a hypothetical situation

steps.m: why not/if only (expressing regret about أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Adding لَآ changes the meaning to express regret or desire about something that didn't happen. Here it's used by the disbelievers to question why a sign wasn't sent down.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

The أُ at the start and the ِ in the middle shows this happened to something rather than something doing it. This word is connected to آيَةٌ (sign) in this ayah, showing the sign is what was sent down.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Added هِ at the end to show this is happening to 'him'
The first root letter أ changes to ء with fatha, and the second root letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter. The third root letter gets a fatha.
Adding ة makes it into a noun that refers to one specific instance of showing or making clear - in this case, a sign or miracle
The tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is the subject of the verb أُنزِلَ (was sent down) in this ayah
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere or something
Added shadda to show strong connection with next word. This preposition connects 'from' to 'his Lord' (رَّبِّهِ) in this ayah.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Adding هِ with the small alif (ۦٓ) shows this belongs to him. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
Adding shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from 'if' to 'indeed/certainly'
Adding ما changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This combination is used to restrict or limit what comes after it.
The alif is written with a long mark (آ) at the end of words in the Quran to show proper pronunciation
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'you' when talking to one male person. In this ayah, it's connected to مُنذِرٌ (warner) to say 'you are a warner.'
Added the vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it: 'anta'

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger
warner (one who warns others about danger)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle changes this from an action into someone who does that action regularly. Like how 'teach' becomes 'teacher' in English.
The ٌ at the end shows we mean any warner in general. This word tells us what 'you' (أَنتَ) are - like saying 'you are a warner.'
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Adding لِ (for/to) makes it show possession or purpose
Adding conjunction وَ to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: to stand firmly, rise up, establish
people who stand together (owned by كُلِّ)
steps.n: Changed verb into noun meaning 'people' - comes from idea of people standing together as one. This word belongs to كُلِّ (every/each) in this ayah.
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is any people/nation in general, not a specific one

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding vowels (َ) and writing ى instead of ي at the end
Changed the word pattern to show who does the action, like changing 'guide' to 'guider'

Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a guide' instead of 'the guide'). The ي drops when we add this mark. This word relates to قَوْمٍ (people) in the phrase 'for every people is a guide.'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added ال with special spelling, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in يَعْلَمُ (He knows)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This shows Allah's continuous knowledge of everything mentioned after this word in the ayah.
Same word with different vowel mark - both mean 'he knows'
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the verb يَعْلَمُ (He knows). In this ayah, it introduces what Allah knows about - specifically what every female carries.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: she carries/bears (subject of what each female carries)
steps.n: Added تَ at start to show 'she' in present tense, changed middle vowel, and added ُ at end. This word connects to أُنثَىٰ (female) to describe what she carries.
Connected the root letters that mean completeness or wholeness
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - changes from 'complete' to 'every/all'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Changes root into a noun meaning 'female' by adding specific vowel marks and ending
Added the alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which shows this is the final form of the word. This is just a spelling convention and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a particle word that means 'what'. In this ayah, it's asking about what the wombs decrease.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This word asks about what the wombs decrease (تَغِيضُ).
Changed the first vowel to kasra to show this happens to something rather than something doing it
Added تَ to show a female is doing the action

steps.m: to show mercy, be compassionate, care deeply

steps.n: Adding vowels َ and ِ makes this a past tense verb about showing mercy
Changes from verb 'showed mercy' to plural noun meaning multiple instances of mercy
Added 'ال' (the) to make it 'the wombs' specifically, and added a damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is doing the action
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. In this ayah, this word is asking 'what' the wombs decrease.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. It connects to the previous phrase about what wombs carry.
Connected root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added تَ prefix to show present tense feminine subject. The middle ا changes back to ي in present tense.
Changed to Form VIII pattern to show the action happens to the subject. The ت changes to د after ز for easier pronunciation.
Present tense verb showing that something/someone (she) is increasing
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added و at the start to mean 'and', and ُ at the end to show this word is the subject of the verb تَغِيضُ (decreases) in this ayah

The root letters ش-ي-أ are made into the noun شَيْء by adding a fatha (short a) on the ش and a sukun (stop mark) on the ي. It means 'a thing' and is owned by the word كُلُّ (every) that comes before it.
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this word belongs to كُلُّ (every/all). The ي changed to ى but this doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make it a preposition
Added هُۥ at the end to show who/what the preposition refers to. This word appears in the phrase 'everything with Him is by due measure', where 'Him' refers to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added مِ prefix and changed vowels to make a noun meaning 'amount/measurement'

steps.m: by/with a measure (describing how everything is with Allah)
steps.n: Added بِ prefix meaning 'with/by' and tanwin at the end to show indefiniteness. This word describes how everything with Allah is according to a precise measure.
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Added alif after first letter to show the doer of the action
Changed alif to alif with bar (عٰ) and added damma at end to show this is one of Allah's names describing His complete knowledge of the unseen and witnessed
Changes from verb 'to be hidden' into noun meaning 'hidden thing'
Adding ال makes it specific ('the unseen'). The kasra (ِ) shows it's possessed by the word before it (عٰلِمُ)
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changes from 'he witnessed' (past action) to 'witnessing' (concept) by adding alif and 'ah' at the end
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and doubles the ش sound
Shows this word is possessed by or connected to what follows it
Adds 'and' to connect with previous word الْغَيْبِ (the unseen), and changes alif to dagger alif for style
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Forms an adjective describing the quality of being great/large
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ى at end to show doing action to oneself
Added مُ at start to make it mean 'the one who' is exalted, changed ending to show it's describing Allah's constant attribute of being high above all
Added ال (the) to show this is a specific divine name of Allah meaning 'The Most High'
Adding fatha marks makes it a past tense verb meaning 'made equal'
Changed from a verb (he made equal) to a noun (equal) by adding alif and hamza at the end
This is a basic word that shows where something comes from or what something is part of, like saying 'from' in English
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after the word سَوَآءٌ

steps.m: from you all (referring to those who hide or reveal their speech)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed. This refers to anyone who might hide or reveal their speech, mentioned in the rest of the ayah.
This is a basic word that means 'whoever' or 'one who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who concealed something.
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the م because it comes after another م in مِّنكُم in the previous word. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to make it 'he kept secret' in the past tense
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make/cause something to be secret'
Root letters ق-و-ل became the noun قَوْل (speech) by adding vowels. This is what someone is hiding/concealing.
Added ال to make it specific ('the speech' instead of just 'speech')
This is a special word used to ask about or refer to people in general. In this ayah, it's the subject of هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍ (he is hiding)
و connects this with the previous part about those who speak openly (جَهَرَ), contrasting different types of people

steps.m: to speak openly or make apparent
he spoke openly (contrasts with أَسَرَّ/spoke secretly)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word contrasts with أَسَرَّ (spoke secretly) earlier in the ayah to show opposite actions.
Basic preposition showing how something is done or what tool is used
Added هِ which refers back to ٱلْقَوْلَ (the speech/saying) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a special word used to talk about people in general. In this ayah, it's connected to هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍ (he who hides) that comes after it.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous one about those who speak openly (وَمَن جَهَرَ بِهِۦ)
This is a pronoun used to refer to one male person or thing. In this ayah, it refers back to مَنْ (whoever) from the previous part, connecting the description of someone who hides or speaks openly.
Basic past tense verb form indicating the state of being hidden
The prefix مُسْتَ shows someone is trying to do the action to themselves
The word changed from a verb (action of hiding) to describing someone who is hiding, shown by the ٍ ending
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun for night
Added 'the' to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because it combines with the ل from ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word بِ (meaning 'in/by/with')
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'in' or 'during'. This word appears in contrast with بِٱلنَّهَارِ (during the day) in the next verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who is doing the action. This pairs with مُسْتَخْفٍ (hiding) in the verse.
Added tanween to show this is indefinite
Added و to connect this with the previous word مُسْتَخْفٍ (hiding)
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed or follows a word like 'in' or 'with'
ال makes it specific and ِ shows it follows a preposition

steps.m: they protect/guard (referring to مُعَقِّبٰتٌ - the guardians)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action

The doubled middle letter shows doing something with more force or repeatedly
Adding يُ at the start shows this is happening in the present/future. The kasra under ي is needed for the doubled letter pattern.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to the conditions or state that people change within themselves.

steps.m: to stand firm, establish, rise up

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/establishing becomes a noun for a group that stands together as one unit
Added بِ to show this is what Allah wills/intends for these people. This word is connected to the verb أَرَادَ (He wills/intends) in this ayah.
The tanween (ٍ) makes the word indefinite (any people) and shows it's affected by the preposition بِ
This is a basic particle that shows a limit or endpoint. It has no root since it's a particle.

Added alif maqsura with superscript alif at the end. This word connects to يُغَيِّرُوا۟ (they change) to show the endpoint of the action.
The doubled middle letter shows doing something with more effort or repeatedly
Adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean 'he changes' (present) instead of 'he changed' (past)

steps.m: they change intensively (subject of حَتَّىٰ - until)
steps.n: Adding وا makes it plural - shows many people doing the action. This verb comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) which requires this special ending.

steps.n: In this ayah, مَا is used to refer to the conditions or state that Allah does not change for a people until they change themselves. It connects the verb يُغَيِّرُ (he changes) with what is being changed.

Changed from singular to plural by adding أ at start and changing vowels - this is a broken plural pattern
Added هِمْ to show possession - means these are their own selves
Added بِ at beginning to show this describes how something is done
This is a conditional word that introduces a time or condition. The ا at the end makes it specifically about future or conditional situations.

steps.m: and when (connecting to previous statement about Allah not changing people's condition)
steps.n: The وَ connects this condition to the previous statement. The آ is just a written variation of ا that shows the same meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it past tense
Adding أَ at start makes it mean 'to make someone want/will something'
Changed the root into a noun for 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of The One True God. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis. The ُ shows this is the doer of the action in the sentence.

steps.m: to stand firmly, establish, rise up

steps.n: Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Adds preposition 'bi' to show 'with' or 'by' the people
Adds tanween (double kasra) to show this is indefinite
Changed the action (to harm) into the thing itself (harm)
The ًا at the end shows this word is receiving the action of أَرَادَ (He willed), like saying "He willed harm"

steps.m: then/so (connecting to previous statement about Allah's will)
steps.n: Starting with the first particle ف which connects this statement to what came before. It shows that this is a result or consequence of the previous statement about Allah's will.

steps.n: Added the negation particle لا which negates the word مَرَدَّ (averting) that comes after it. Together they mean 'so there is no averting it'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
When two same letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Adding مَ at the start changes this from an action to a place or time when that action happens. In this ayah, it refers to there being no way to turn back Allah's decree once He wills something.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ which means 'him' to show who the action is for. This word connects to مُعَقِّبٰتٌ (angels who protect) in this ayah, showing who they protect.
Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (for questions/statements) or 'not' (for negation). In this ayah, it means 'not' because it's being used with لَهُم to say 'they do not have'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added هُم which means 'them' to show who the action is for
This is the basic form of the word that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something

steps.m: from/of (after a word ending in ن or م)
steps.n: The shadda (double mark) on م shows it blends with the previous word's ending. This only happens when the previous word ends in ن or م, not ت.

steps.m: to be beneath or lower than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath'
Added kasra because it follows the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah
Added هِۦ at the end to show this refers to Allah

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

The ٍ (tanween kasra) at the end shows this is indefinite (like 'a/any') and comes after the word مِن in the ayah
This is the basic form of the pronoun that means 'he' or 'it'

steps.m: he/it (independent form that stands alone) (subject of يُرِيكُمُ - shows you)
steps.n: Adding وَ makes this an independent pronoun that can stand alone at the start of a sentence. In this ayah, it refers to Allah who is the one showing the lightning.
This is the base word that means 'who' or 'which'. The ى at the end shows it's connecting to something
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific. This word connects هُوَ (He) to what comes after it, telling us more about who 'He' is.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding kasra makes it mean 'to cause to see/to show' instead of just 'to see'

steps.m: he shows you all (البَرْقَ - the lightning)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show who is being shown the lightning - 'you all' (masculine plural)
Changes from action to the thing that flashes. This is what Allah is showing us in this verse.

steps.m: the lightning (what Allah shows in يُرِيكُمُ)
steps.n: Added ال (the) at start and َ at end because it's the direct object of 'shows'

steps.m: fear (object of يُرِيكُمُ - He shows you)
steps.n: Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the feeling of fear. This word is what is being shown in the phrase 'He shows you.'

steps.m: fearfully (describing how He shows you the lightning)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing how the lightning is shown. It answers the question 'How does He show you the lightning?'

steps.m: and He creates/brings forth (subject is هُوَ)
steps.n: وَ connects it to what came before

Added هِ with a small ۦ to show this refers to Him (Allah)

steps.n: Added كَ at beginning to show comparison, and kasra at end to connect to following word. This describes someone trying to grasp water, showing the futility of their action.

steps.m: his two palms (like one stretching out)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show these are his palms. In this ayah, this is part of a metaphor comparing those who call upon false gods to someone stretching out their palms toward water.

steps.m: to call upon or pray to someone

Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence

This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows the disbelievers are deep within misguidance.
