Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This word connects to يَسْجُدُ (prostrates) in the ayah, showing to whom the prostration is done.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on س, and damma on ج. This word shows who is doing the prostrating that belongs to Allah (وَلِلَّهِ)
Present tense verb meaning 'he prostrates/bows down' - subject of the sentence
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to people or things in a general way. In this ayah, it refers to those who prostrate (يَسْجُدُ).
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun for the thing that is high up
Added ات to make it plural, with و added between for smooth pronunciation
Added ال to make it specific, with shadda on س because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word في (in). The alifs are written as long alifs (ٰ) in this style.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters together
The و connects this word to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it
The kasra shows this word is connected to السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) in a pair
Root letters get vowel marks to become a noun meaning 'willing obedience' - like turning 'obey' into 'obedience'
Adding ًا makes it describe how the action is done, connecting it to يَسْجُدُ (prostrates) in this ayah
Changes verb 'to hate' into the noun 'hatred'
Adding tanween alif makes this describe how the action happens - it shows that the prostration is done in a state of unwillingness
Adding و connects this with طَوْعًا (willingly) to show contrast between willing and unwilling prostration
Connected the root letters and added kasra and shadda to make the basic noun form
Changes from single shadow to many shadows by adding alif and changing the middle vowel
Added damma to show this word is doing the action of prostrating
Added هُم at the end to show these are 'their' shadows

steps.m: and their shadows (that prostrate to Allah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added damma vowels to create the noun form
Added shadda to strengthen the weak letter و
Added the definite article ال to make it specific
Added preposition ب meaning 'in' and changed alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ)
Root meaning 'deep/firm' became a time word meaning late afternoon (when shadows are long/deep)
Changed to broken plural form for multiple late afternoons
Added 'the' to make it specific and added kasra at end to show it's connected to what comes before it
Added 'and' to connect it with the previous word الغدو (the early mornings)
The word has the same meaning - the kasra (small diagonal line) at the end shows it belongs to/is owned by something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) Dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) Adding damma (ُ) on first letter, 3) Adding sukun (ْ) on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers about who is the Lord of the heavens and earth.

steps.m: who (asking about the Lord of the heavens and earth)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about people. In this ayah, it's asking 'who is the Lord of the heavens and earth?' The answer comes right after: 'Say: Allah.'

steps.m: to care for, nurture, be lord over

steps.n: Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatha) to each root letter turns it into a past tense verb meaning 'he nurtured'
Double letter with shadda shows intensity of the quality. Changed from action to title of one who does the action.

steps.m: Lord (subject of السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added shadda at start for emphasis and damma at end to show this is the subject. This word is connected to 'heavens and earth' showing He is Lord over them.
Root meaning 'high' becomes 'sky' by adding اء - since the sky is high above us
Changes sky/heaven into its plural form by adding -waat at the end
Makes the word definite by adding 'the' at the start
Adds kasra to show this word is possessed by رَبُّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of the heavens'
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters

steps.m: and the earth (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word is connected to something else in the sentence
The root letters ق-و-ل become قُل when making a command to one person. The و drops out and we add a dot (damma) above the ق to make it a command.

steps.m: say! (commanding one person to tell others about Allah)
steps.n: The kasra is added at the end because the next word starts with a hamza (ٱللَّهُ). This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Changed from basic root to command form by adding damma (ُ) - like telling someone 'Say this!'
The middle letter و is dropped and replaced with a sukun (ْ) because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Basic root meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ت with shadda to show doing something for yourself
Added تُم at the end to show many people (you all) did this action

steps.m: did you all take for yourselves?
steps.n: Adding أ at start turns this into a question - 'did you?'
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
Added shadda to show this word connects strongly to what comes after it. Here it connects to دُونِهِۦٓ (besides Him) to mean "from besides Him"
Basic root meaning
Adding damma (ُ) makes it into a descriptive word meaning 'lower'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is connected to something that comes after it
Adding هِ with the elongation mark ۦٓ shows this refers to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to protect, to be close to and take care of

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'protect' to noun meaning 'protector' by adding vowels and doubling last letter
Changes from single protector to many protectors by using the plural pattern أَفْعِلَاء

steps.m: protectors, guardians (as objects of اتَّخَذْتُم - you have taken)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'you have taken' (اتَّخَذْتُم)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. Here it makes 'they possess' into 'they do not possess'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The يَ at the start and the vowel pattern change shows it's happening in the present/future
Adding ون shows multiple subjects (in this case, the false deities/protectors mentioned in the ayah that people take besides Allah)
The root letters are connected with vowels to form a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'. This represents something precious and valuable - a person's essence.
Changes the singular 'self' to plural 'selves' by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels. This is a special plural form used for words that mean 3-10 of something.
Adds لِ at the beginning to show these selves are receiving something. This connects to the verb يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess) in the verse, showing what they don't possess benefit for.

steps.m: for/to their selves (objects that يَمْلِكُونَ don't benefit)
steps.n: The هِمْ shows these are their own selves that they can't benefit. In the verse, this emphasizes that these false gods can't even help themselves, let alone others.
Connected the root letters together and added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun. This word is what the protectors they took besides Allah cannot do for themselves.
Added ًا at the end to show this is something that the false protectors cannot do for themselves, matching with لَا يَمْلِكُونَ (they do not possess/control)
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَمْلِكُونَ (they possess) that comes after it in the ayah
The و connects this negation to the previous phrase about not possessing benefit (نَفْعًا), extending the negation to harm (ضَرًّا) as well
The shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter ر to show the harm is strong or intense
Adding ًا changes this from 'harming' to 'harm' - now it's the thing that they cannot possess
Makes it into a command to speak
This is a command form (do this!) telling one person to speak - no letters were dropped here
This is a question word used to ask yes/no questions. In this ayah, it's asking if two things are equal (يَسْتَوِى).
Added short vowels (fat-ha, kasra, fat-ha) to the root letters to make a past tense verb meaning 'became equal'
Added است at beginning to show seeking or achieving a state completely
Changed to present tense and added subject pronoun. This verb compares if the blind and the seeing are equal in the ayah.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to pattern أَفْعَل to create an adjective meaning 'blind'

steps.m: the blind one (contrasted with ٱلْبَصِيرُ/the seeing one)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and alif maksura at the end. This word is being contrasted with ٱلْبَصِيرُ (the seeing one) in this ayah to show the difference between those who see the truth and those who don't.

steps.m: one who sees/perceives deeply (opposite of الْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: Changes the word from an action to describe someone who does that action very well. This word is being contrasted with الْأَعْمَىٰ (the blind person) in this ayah.

steps.m: the one who sees/perceives deeply (opposite of الْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific/definite, opposite of 'blind' in this ayah

steps.m: and the one who sees/perceives deeply (opposite of الْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: The و at the start means 'and'

steps.m: or (connecting two equal questions: 'Are the blind and seeing equal OR have they made partners...')
steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two equal alternatives. In this ayah, it connects two questions: one about blindness/sight and another about making partners with Allah.
This is a question word that turns the statement 'they are equal' into the question 'are they equal?' It's like adding a question mark and moving 'is/are' to the front in English.
Added ت to show seeking or requesting equality
Added ت to make it reflexive - something becoming equal on its own
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
The word became plural and feminine by changing the vowels to damma and adding ات
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added the nominative case ending ُ because this word is the subject of تَسْتَوِى (becomes equal/same). Also changed alif to alif madd (ٰ) which is common in Quranic spelling.
Changed the action of shining into the noun 'light' by adding ُ (damma) on the first letter and و after it
Doubling the middle letter adds intensity to the meaning
Makes the noun definite
Added وَ (and) at the beginning and ُ at the end. This word is being contrasted with 'darkness' in this ayah.

steps.m: or (connecting two questions about هَلْ تَسْتَوِى)
steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two similar questions. In this ayah, it connects 'are the blind and seeing equal?' with 'are darkness and light equal?' Both questions start with هَلْ تَسْتَوِى (are they equal?).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense. This word acts on شُرَكَآءَ (partners) in this ayah.
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This refers to those who set up partners with Allah.
This is the base word for God's proper name in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
Added لِ at the beginning to show this belongs to or is for Allah. The initial ا is dropped when لِ is added.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the verb جَعَلُوا (they made/set up) in the ayah.

steps.m: to share or join with others

steps.n: Added ي between second and third letters to show someone who does the action
Changed the first vowel from 'a' to 'u', middle vowel from 'i' to 'a', and added اء at the end to make it plural (like changing 'partner' to 'partners')
Changed alif to long alif and added fatha at end to show this word is the object of جَعَلُوا (they made/set up)

steps.m: to create, make, or bring into existence

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This is the most basic past tense form.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation mark.

steps.m: to create or form something new
creation (the act or result of creating)
steps.n: Root meaning 'create' becomes a noun 'creation' by adding the vowel marks fatHa and sukoon
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added كَ at the beginning to show comparison
Added هِۦ at the end to show this refers to Allah's creation
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ا after first root letter to show the action happens by itself

steps.m: and so it became similar (to خَلْقِهِۦ - His creation)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this happens as a result of what came before. The word relates to خَلْقِهِۦ (His creation) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that means the process or result of creating something. This word appears in a comparison with خَلْقِهِ (His creation) in this ayah.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being discussed. This word is being compared to Allah's creation (خَلْقِهِ) in this ayah.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon them (refers to the false partners/شُرَكَآءَ mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to 'them' (referring back to the false partners mentioned earlier in the verse)
The middle weak letter و is dropped and the vowels are changed to make it a command form
The kasra is added because this word is connected to اللَّهُ (Allah) which comes right after it
Changed إِلَه to اللَه by adding ال and merging letters. This makes it a proper name for The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the answer to 'Who is the Lord of the heavens and earth?')
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to show emphasis, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the subject answering the question in the ayah
Added alif after first letter to show who does the creating. This word is part of the phrase 'creator of all things'.
Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this describes Allah in this sentence
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) on ل makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the phrase 'of every thing'
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'thing'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this thing is owned by كُلِّ (every/all). The ي is written as ى which is just a spelling variation.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he'. The ُ shows it's a subject pronoun, and the َ helps connect it to other words.
Added و with fatha at the start to mean 'and'. This connects 'he' to the following words ٱلْوٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ (the One, the Irresistible).
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed to show someone or something that has this quality of being one/unique

steps.m: The One (a name of Allah showing His absolute uniqueness)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's describing Allah. This is one of Allah's names, showing He is absolutely One with no partners.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The doubled ه and ار ending create intensive active participle meaning 'one who does this extensively'

steps.m: The All-Dominant (one of Allah's names showing His complete power over all creation)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific to Allah, and the ُ shows this is describing Him
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is past tense

steps.m: he sent down (referring to Allah sending down rain)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes someone cause the action instead of doing it themselves - like changing 'came down' to 'made come down' or 'sent down'
This is the basic preposition that shows where something came from or its origin
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) which requires this ending. Also lengthened the alif to آ.
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to make a past-tense verb
The root word changed to make the special word for water - the middle and end letters changed to make it easier to say

steps.m: water (object of أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Added ً (tanween) at the end to show this is the object being sent down from the sky. Also added آ (long alif) to show the extended sound.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added ت with sukun to show feminine past tense

steps.m: and then she/it flowed (after مَآءً water)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened after the water was sent down
Connected the root letters and added alif to form the basic word for 'valley'
Changes from single valley to multiple valleys by adding أ at the start and changing the middle letters - this is a broken plural pattern
Adding double dammah makes the word indefinite - like saying 'some valleys' instead of 'the valleys'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic noun meaning 'measure' or 'amount'
Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show how something is done
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: by its measure (referring to the valleys)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show this refers to the valleys (أَوْدِيَةٌ) mentioned earlier - each valley flows according to its own capacity
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added اِحْتَ at the beginning to show the action happens to itself - like something picking itself up

steps.m: and so it carried itself (subject of السَّيْلُ/the flood)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of what came before. This word is describing what السَّيْلُ (the flood) did.

steps.m: a flood, torrent (single instance of flowing)
steps.n: Changes from verb 'to flow' into noun meaning 'a flood'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific flood. When ال is added before س, the ل combines with س to make a stronger س sound (shown by ّ).
The ُ shows this word is doing the action of carrying (احْتَمَلَ)
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make it a noun

steps.m: foam (object being carried by السَّيْلُ/the flood)
steps.n: The -an ending shows this is the object (the foam) being carried by the flood
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from verb (it rose) to describing word (rising)
Adds -an ending to show it's describing something in a special way (accusative case)
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'from'
The word 'min' joins with 'ma' (what), and the 'n' merges into 'm' making it stronger
Adding the conjunction و at the beginning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added ون ending to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun suffix
Added هِ at the end to show 'him/it' - the thing being acted upon

This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects the word النَّارِ (the fire) to the verb يُوقِدُونَ (they kindle/ignite), showing where the action happens.
The middle letter و changes to ا, and we add fatha on the first letter to make the noun for 'fire'
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to something after it
Adding ال makes the word specific/definite
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first letter to show doing action for oneself with effort
Changed to noun form showing the act of seeking itself
Changed spelling to match Quranic style and added a mark at the end to show this is the thing being sought
Changed the verb into a noun for a single piece of jewelry or ornament
Added tanwin (double kasra) to show this is the object being sought. This word is connected to ابْتِغَآءَ (seeking) in the ayah.
This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between options. Here it connects two things people seek from fire: jewelry (حِلْيَة) or useful items (متٰع).
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to connect the root letters and turn the verb root into a noun meaning 'something beneficial'
Added alif to make it into a noun meaning the result or object of the action
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a/some' in English) and is being acted upon by the word ابْتِغَآءَ (seeking) that comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun form
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject doing the action. In this ayah, this foam/scum is what 'goes away' (يَذْهَبُ)

steps.m: to be similar to, to resemble

steps.n: Adding the kasra (ِ) changes this from a verb into a noun meaning 'similar to'
Adds a u-sound at the end to show it's the subject or starts a sentence
Adding attached pronoun هُۥ meaning 'it' referring back to the foam mentioned earlier
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذٰ (that) to specify what is being compared to
Added لِ to emphasize the comparison
Added كَ at the end to address the listener, making the comparison more direct
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. In this ayah, Allah (اللَّهُ) is presenting parables to teach people.
Changed the root into a noun form meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'

Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال. The ل is made stronger with a double mark (shadda) to show this is the One True God. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing something in this sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them pronounceable
Doubled the middle letter to add intensity to the meaning
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of يَضْرِبُ (strikes/presents)
Changes from verb 'to be false' to noun meaning 'that which is false'
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Added َ at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَضْرِبُ (strikes/compares)
This is a basic particle that introduces a topic that will be contrasted with something else later in the text

steps.m: then as for (connects to فَيَذْهَبُ - then it goes away)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before. In this ayah, it connects to فَيَذْهَبُ (then it goes away) which comes after.

steps.m: to foam or rise to the surface

steps.n: Root letters turned into a simple noun meaning 'foam' - like when soap bubbles rise to the top of water
Adding 'the' and damma ending because this foam is the subject doing the action of 'going away' (يَذْهَبُ) in the next ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Adds يَ at start and changes ending to ُ to make it present tense 'he goes' instead of past tense 'he went'

steps.m: then he goes away/vanishes (describing what happens to الزَّبَدُ - the foam)
steps.n: The فَ (fa) at the start means 'then/so', showing what happens next to the foam
Added basic vowel pattern to connect letters
Changed from a verb (floating) to a noun (foam) by stretching the middle sound and adding hamza at the end
Added -an ending to show it's being used like 'as foam' or 'like foam' (describes the state of something)
This is a particle that introduces a new topic or contrasts with something previously mentioned. It's often used with فَ later in the sentence to complete the meaning.

steps.m: and as for (contrasts with فَأَمَّا in previous ayah)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This creates a parallel structure with فَأَمَّا from the previous ayah, setting up a comparison.

steps.m: that which (refers to what benefits people in يَنفَعُ ٱلنَّاسَ)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it refers to the beneficial things mentioned in the next phrase يَنفَعُ ٱلنَّاسَ (benefits people).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The يَ at the beginning shows it's happening now, and the ُ at the end shows this is the main verb. This word tells us what benefits 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسَ) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun for people who do this action - people are social beings who live together

steps.m: the people (object of يَنفَعُ - what benefits)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of 'benefits'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to show ongoing action
Added فَ to show this happens as a result of what came before in the ayah
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it specifically shows that something is happening in or within the fire.

steps.m: to spread out wide, to expand

steps.n: The root letters أ-ر-ض with vowel marks (فتحة and سكون) form the simple noun meaning 'earth/land'
Shows this word is being affected by something (in this case, the word فِى meaning 'in')

steps.m: the earth/land (being acted upon by فِى)
steps.n: Adds definite article prefix to make the noun definite
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity
Added ذٰ which is a shortened form of ذا meaning 'this'

steps.m: like that (referring to the previous example in the ayah)
steps.n: Added لِكَ which changes 'this' to 'that' and makes it point back to what was just mentioned. In this ayah, it refers to the example Allah just gave about the foam on water.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowel pattern. Allah (اللَّهُ) is doing this action in the ayah.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only true deity

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَضْرِبُ - He sets forth)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is the one doing the action (setting forth the example) in this verse. The alif at the start is written with a special symbol called a wasla.

steps.m: to compare or be like something
steps.n: This root is about similarity and comparison
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it into a noun meaning 'example'
Changes the word to show many examples instead of just one
Adds ال to show we're talking about specific examples

steps.m: the examples (object of يَضْرِبُ - sets forth)
steps.n: Adds a fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَضْرِبُ (sets forth)
This is the basic word used to point to someone or something specific. The ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'.
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one person

Added ى to make it into a relative pronoun. This turns a simple pointer word into one that connects different parts of a sentence.

Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at beginning makes it causative (Form IV), showing someone made the revelation happen
Adding نا at the end shows 'we' did the action

Basic preposition meaning 'towards' or 'to'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added the pronoun ك meaning 'you' at the end to show who is being addressed
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they', and sukun (ْ) shows the end of the word
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word is the subject of يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve) in this ayah.

steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers back to الرَّحْمٰنِ (The Most Merciful) mentioned in the previous part of the ayah. It's used to emphasize that He alone is the speaker's Lord.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something
steps.n: Basic meaning of nurturing or caring for something

steps.m: lord (one who takes care of others)

Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of لَا (no) in the phrase لَا إِلٰهَ (there is no deity)

This is a standalone pronoun that doesn't come from a root. It's one of the basic building blocks of Arabic. In this ayah, it's being used to identify Allah as 'my Lord' in the statement 'He is my Lord'.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects 'I relied' (تَوَكَّلْتُ) with what is being relied upon.
Added هِ at the end which means 'Him' here, referring to Allah mentioned as رَبِّى (my Lord) in the previous part. The ي is added between عَلَى and هِ to connect them smoothly.

Adding the vowels creates the basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at the beginning and doubling the middle letter changes 'entrust' to 'put complete trust in Allah', showing strong reliance on Him
Changed from past tense 'he trusted' to command form 'trust!' by putting a stop mark (sukun) at the end instead of a vowel
Adding تُ at the end shows that 'I' did this action in the past
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. Since it's a particle, we start with its simplest form rather than a root.
Changed the ending alif to yaa with sukun to prepare for the attached pronoun

steps.m: and to him/it (connected to مَتَابِ - return)

steps.m: to turn back or return (especially to Allah in repentance)

Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it

Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning with/by
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show it refers back to the Quran mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: the mountains (subject of سُيِّرَتْ - were moved)

steps.m: or (connecting قُطِّعَتْ with كُلِّمَ as alternative possibilities)
steps.n: This is a coordinating particle that connects two alternative possibilities in this ayah: either the earth being cut or the dead being spoken to. It shows these are different examples of the same type of miracle.

steps.m: to cut or break into pieces

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added shadda to show intensive cutting into multiple pieces

Added marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the basic word for 'earth'

steps.m: the earth (subject of being split/torn apart)

This is the basic meaning of speaking or talking

steps.m: with/by means of it (referring to قُرْءَانًا)
steps.n: The هِ at the end means 'it' and refers back to the Quran (قُرْءَانًا) mentioned earlier in the ayah. It shows what would be used to move the mountains.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'death'
Forms plural noun meaning 'dead ones'
Added 'the' to make it specific and added the small alif (ٰ) at the end which helps with pronunciation

steps.m: Rather/instead (contrasting with the previous statement about moving mountains)

Added kasra at the end to show possession or direction
Added لِ at beginning to show purpose or direction

Changed the verb into a noun meaning the command itself or the matter being commanded

Added alif after first letter to change meaning from just trusting to actively believing
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action, with optional elongation (ٓ) and silent marker (۟)

The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show this is an important condition
The sukun (ْ) shows where to stop when pronouncing the word

steps.m: to guide, lead, or show the right way

Added لَ at start to emphasize that this definitely happened
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.n: Changes from action to noun meaning 'speech'. This word is part of the phrase ظٰهِرٍ مِّنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ meaning 'manifest/apparent speech'.
The word gets 'the' (ال) at the start and a kasra (ِ) ending because it comes after مِنَ (from/of) and is connected to ظٰهِرٍ (apparent)

steps.m: rather/but rather (corrects the previous statement about شُرَكَآءَ partners)
steps.n: This is a particle that corrects or contradicts what came before. Here it's correcting the false claim about Allah having partners.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb

Changed to passive voice with damma at start and kasra in middle, plus shadda for emphasis. This shows the action was done to someone/something rather than them doing it. In this ayah, it refers to how something was made to seem attractive to those who disbelieved.
The base word is a relative pronoun meaning 'those who'. The ٱ (alif) at the start shows it's connected to the word before it.

steps.m: for those who (connected to مَكْرُهُمْ - their plot)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this word is connected to مَكْرُهُمْ (their plot) in the next part of the ayah, showing whose plot it is.

steps.m: to reject, cover up, deny truth

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he rejected'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Root م-ك-ر became a noun مَكْرُ (plot/scheme) with damma ending to show it's the subject doing the action

steps.m: their plot (that was made attractive to them)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show the plot belongs to them (the disbelievers mentioned in the verse)
Connected the root letters and added basic past tense vowels with shadda on middle letter to show intensity
Changed from 'he blocked' to 'was blocked' by putting a damma (u sound) on first letter to show something was done to it
Adds plural masculine suffix to make verb apply to 'they'

steps.m: and they were blocked/prevented (from السَّبِيلِ - the path)
steps.n: The و (wa) was added at the start to mean 'and', connecting this action to what came before it
This is a basic preposition that shows movement away from something or separation
Added kasra at the end to connect it to the next word السَّبِيلِ (the path). Together they mean 'away from the path'
Added vowels and the long vowel ي to create the basic noun form
Added ال to make it definite, which causes the س to double (shown by shadda). Added kasra at the end to show it's possessed by or connected to something else in the sentence.
This is a combination of 'wa' (and) with 'man' (whoever/who)
This root carries the basic meaning of being lost or straying from the right path
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding أ at the beginning makes the action affect others - like making someone else go astray instead of going astray yourself

steps.m: He (Allah) lets go astray (whoever He wills)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with يُ at start, and added ِ at end to show connection to next word. In this ayah, it refers to Allah letting those who choose disbelief go astray.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
The word for 'a god' becomes the special name 'Allah' by adding ال (the) and doubling the L sound. This makes it mean 'The One True God' instead of just any god.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: then what/then not (connects to يُضْلِلِ - leads astray)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. In this ayah, it's asking 'then what?' about someone who Allah leads astray.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added the pronoun هُ meaning 'him' to show who the action is for
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is a pronunciation helper showing that the هُ should be pronounced with a long 'u' sound
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it connects the word هَادٍ (guide) to show that someone is without/away from guidance.

steps.m: to guide, show the right path

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense verb (guided) to active participle (guide/guider) by adding alif after first letter and changing yaa to kasra-i

steps.m: a guide (after مِنْ in this ayah)
steps.n: The ي is replaced with tanween kasra because it comes after the word مِنْ (from/of) in this ayah. This shows it's indefinite (like saying 'a guide' instead of 'the guide')
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added pronoun suffix هُمْ to show who the preposition refers to
Added emphasis to the preposition with shadda. This word connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in this ayah, showing who will receive the punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun by adding alif after the second root letter. This makes it mean the result of the action.
The double dammah (tanween) shows this is the subject of the sentence and is indefinite (like saying 'a punishment' instead of 'the punishment')
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. Here it connects 'punishment' (عَذَابٌ) with 'the worldly life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ), showing where the punishment occurs.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
This root represents the basic concept of living or being alive
Added و and ة to form the complete noun for 'life'
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ِ to show it's connected to the word الدُّنْيَا (worldly/this world) that follows it

steps.m: to be close or near to something

steps.n: The root meaning 'near' was turned into the word for 'worldly life' or 'this world' - called dunya because it's closer to us in time than the afterlife

Adding ال makes it specific. The ل combines with د making it stronger (shadda). This word describes 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةِ) in this ayah, meaning the life that is closer to us (this world) as opposed to the afterlife.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed verb into noun meaning the result of the action
Added لَ to emphasize the certainty of the punishment

steps.m: and surely the punishment (of الْآخِرَةِ - the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added و to connect to previous statement and ُ to show this is the subject of أَشَقُّ (more difficult) in this ayah

steps.m: to come later, to be delayed

steps.n: Changes root into word meaning 'later/last' by adding alif and kasra pattern

steps.m: the life after this one, the hereafter
steps.n: Adds feminine ending to create a specific noun referring to the afterlife
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific, with a different ending because another word owns it
Adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he split'
Adding 'أ' at start and changing vowels makes it mean 'more' of the root meaning
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (there is for them from اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This negates having any protection from Allah.
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added هُم to show this is happening to them. This word connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in this ayah, showing who the punishment is for.
This is the basic form of the word meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show importance or certainty
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word grammatically to Allah (اللَّهِ) that follows it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added ال (the) at the start, doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show possession. This word specifically refers to the one true God in Islam.
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or separation. Here it connects with وَاقٍ (protector) to show they have no protector from Allah.
Added ا after و to make it mean 'one who does the action', like changing 'protect' to 'protector' in English
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is talking about any protector in general, not a specific one. This comes after the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Root letters 'm-th-l' became the noun 'mathal' by adding 'a' vowels, changing it from 'to be like' into 'an example'
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added shadda on first letter to make the meaning stronger
Added vowels and shadda to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: garden (a place that is covered/concealed by trees)
steps.n: The ة at the end makes this into a noun meaning 'a place that is covered', which came to mean 'garden' because gardens are covered by trees
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific, and the ِ shows it's connected to the word before it (مَّثَلُ). In this context, it refers specifically to Paradise.
This is the basic feminine relative pronoun. The ى at the end is just a spelling variation of ي

steps.m: the one who/which (feminine) (describes الْجَنَّةِ - the garden)
steps.n: Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it definite. This word connects back to 'the garden' (الْجَنَّةِ) and introduces more information about it
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone received the promise rather than made it

steps.m: to guard against harm or evil

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (َ) to make it mean 'he guarded/protected'
Added مُ at start to show who does the action, and تَّ in middle to show doing it to oneself. The و from root combines with ت making it stronger (ّ)
Added ون to show this refers to many people who guard against evil
Added ٱلْ to show these are specific God-fearing people being promised Paradise in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
تَ at start shows this is happening now (present tense)
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it shows the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
Basic concept of being underneath something
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to something after it

steps.m: beneath it (referring to الْجَنَّةِ - the garden)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this refers back to the garden mentioned earlier
Changed from a root meaning 'to flow' into a noun meaning 'river/stream'
Changed from verb 'to flow' to the noun 'river' by making middle letter silent
Changed from single river to plural by adding 'a' at start and 'aa' in middle - this is a common plural pattern
Added definite article
Changed the ending to show it's the subject (doer) of the sentence, with a small spelling change in the middle
The root 'eat' becomes a plural noun 'foods' by adding the u-vowels (the little loops) above the letters
Shows this word is the subject that is being described as permanent/lasting (دَآئِمٌ) in the ayah

steps.m: its food/sustenance (referring to الْجَنَّةِ - the garden/paradise)
steps.n: Adds feminine possessive pronoun هَا referring back to الْجَنَّةِ (the garden/paradise) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Root letters د-و-م got vowels (َ ِ) to make an active participle meaning 'continuing'
Changed the و to ا to make it mean 'something that lasts forever'
Added tanween damma at the end to show this is describing something in a general way. Also changed اَ to آ for proper writing.
Added kasra and doubled the last letter to make it into a noun meaning the shade itself
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added ها to show this shade belongs to something (referring back to the garden/paradise)

steps.m: and its shade (connected to أُكُلُهَا - its food)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous word أُكُلُهَا (its food), showing that both the food and shade of Paradise are being described
The تِ is the basic feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to things
Adding لْ changes it from pointing to something close to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (feminine, far) (pointing to عُقْبَى - the end result)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it stronger and more specific. In this ayah, it's pointing to عُقْبَى (the end result) that will be mentioned next.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'what comes after'
Changed the vowels and added ى at the end to create a feminine noun meaning 'final result'. This word describes what happens to الْكٰفِرِينَ (the disbelievers) in this ayah.
The base word ذا (this) becomes ذي (who/which) to create a relative pronoun that connects descriptions to things
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people or things
Adding ال makes it point to specific people or things. The shadda emphasizes the connection. In this ayah, it refers to those who were mindful of Allah (ٱتَّقَوا۟)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added ٱتّ at the beginning to make it mean 'protecting oneself' instead of 'protecting others' - like adding 'self' in English
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'what follows/comes after' by adding damma and sukun marks
Added ى at the end to make it into an abstract concept
Added و with fatha to connect this word to the previous phrase
Word is now complete with و conjunction prefix

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does this action
Made plural to show multiple people
Added 'Al' (the) at the start and changed the ending to show it's connected to 'end/outcome' in the sentence
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) between the root letters to make the simple noun meaning 'light'
Changed weak letter و to ا and added tanween damma at end
Adds nominative case marker to show this is the subject
Adding ال makes it specific - talking about a particular fire
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that points to someone or something
Added ن at the end to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it point to specific people, and added shadda on ل to connect the sounds
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Changes from basic 'come' to 'make something come/give'
Adding نا changes it from 'he gave' to 'we gave'
Added هُمُ at the end to show who received the action. This word connects to الْكِتٰبَ (the book) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wrote' to 'book' by changing middle vowel to 'i' and adding long 'aa' sound
The ٱلْ makes it 'the book' instead of just any book, and the َ at the end shows it's being acted upon
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is something that happened in the past
Changes from past tense (he felt joy) to present tense (he feels joy) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they (those who were given the Book) feel joy/happiness
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end, connecting back to 'those given the Book'
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of speech. Here it connects to the verb أُنزِلَ (was sent down) that follows it.
The alif is written longer (آ instead of ا) because it comes at the end of a word part. This doesn't change the meaning.
The prefix بِ (meaning 'with') is added to show what they are rejoicing with/about. This connects back to يَفْرَحُونَ (they rejoice).
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: to cause to descend, send down
steps.n: Adding أ at the beginning makes someone cause the action - someone makes something come down
Changed vowels to show this is happening to something rather than something doing it. This word is what they (يَفْرَحُونَ) are rejoicing about.
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added the pronoun كَ which means 'you' to show who the action is directed towards
Added fatha at the end to help connect with the next word (الأحزاب) smoothly in pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Root letters ح-ز-ب were joined with vowels to make the word 'party/group' - something that gathers together
Changed from single party to multiple parties by adding 'a' at start and 'aa' in middle - this is a common plural pattern
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular groups
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by مِنَ (from) in the verse
This is a word that refers to any person who does something. In this ayah, it refers to whoever denies or rejects (يُنكِرُ) part of what was revealed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end
The root letters get vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make a noun meaning 'a part' or 'some'. This word is what's being denied in the sentence.

steps.m: a part, portion (acted upon by يُنكِرُ - denies)
steps.n: The word gets a fatHa mark at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb 'denies'

steps.m: part of it (acted upon by يُنكِرُ - denies)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show this means 'of it'. The ۥ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding damma at start and sukun at end. The middle weak letter و is dropped in this form.
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'
Adding ما changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This combination creates a limiting or restrictive meaning.
The alif is written with a vertical line (آ) at the end of words. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Basic past tense form with fatha vowels
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: I was commanded (in response to قُلْ - he said)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' was commanded. This is what the speaker (Muhammad ﷺ) was told to say after قُلْ (he said).

steps.m: that (connecting أُمِرْتُ 'I was commanded' to أَعْبُدَ 'I worship')
steps.n: This particle connects two ideas by introducing what someone was commanded to do. Here it connects 'I was commanded' (أُمِرْتُ) with 'I worship' (أَعْبُدَ), showing what the command was about.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: that I worship (after أُمِرْتُ أَنْ - I was commanded that)
steps.n: Changed to present tense first person ('I') form with أَ prefix. The sukun and damma show it's in the subjunctive mood because it comes after أَنْ (that). This connects to the previous phrase 'I was commanded that...'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'al' to make it definite - like changing 'god' to 'the god'

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَعْبُدَ - I worship)
steps.n: The word for 'god' (ilah) became the special name 'Allah', with a fatha mark at the end showing Allah is the one being worshipped
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation of the negative word
Extended the alif to emphasize the negation

steps.m: and not (connects to أُمِرْتُ - I was commanded)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about what the speaker was commanded to do
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added هِ to show who/what is being referred to
Added ۦٓ which is just a pronunciation mark to show the vowel is lengthened slightly
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Changed ى to ي to prepare for adding the pronoun suffix
Added هِ (him/it) at the end. This word connects to أَدْعُوا۟ (I call/invite) in the next part of the ayah, showing who is being called to.
Added fathas and the weak letter و changes to ا at the end
Added أ to make it first person ('I') and changed middle vowel to damma
Added ا at the end to clearly show it's first person ('I')
Starting with the basic preposition إِلَى which means 'to'
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attachment of pronoun
Added هِ at the end to show 'him/it'
Added وَ at beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to أَدْعُوا۟ (I call/invite) in the ayah.

steps.m: to return or come back to a place

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens
Added آ to show the meaning of returning or going back

steps.m: place of return (connected to إِلَيْهِ - to Him)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to إِلَيْهِ (to Him) in the ayah
This is the base demonstrative word meaning 'that'
Added ك at the beginning to show comparison or similarity
Added ك at the end to show this is directed to you
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adding hamza at the start makes the verb causative - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is making it come down. This is a verb, so it doesn't have case endings like nouns do.
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action. This is a verb, not a noun, so it doesn't have case endings.
Adding ه shows what was sent down (it/him). This is a complete verb form with its pronoun object attached, not in a case relationship with other words.

steps.m: to decide or make wise judgments

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'judge' into the noun 'judgment' by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon)
Added ًا at the end to show this judgment is being acted upon - in this case, it's what was 'sent down' (أَنزَلْنٰهُ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the base noun
Forms an adjective meaning 'relating to Arab/Arabic'
Added -an ending to show this word describes حُكْمًا (judgment/ruling) in the ayah
Starting with the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added لَ to add emphasis, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly'
Changed إِ to ئِ because it comes after a letter with a vowel mark
Added نِ at the end for extra emphasis. This word introduces a condition that connects to the next word ٱتَّبَعْتَ (you followed)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added alif with kasra at start and shadda on middle letter to show intensive following
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action in the past
Changed initial alif-kasra to alif-wasla for pronunciation
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks (َ). The ى (alif maqsurah) at the end shows it's a past tense verb, like many Arabic verbs that end this way
Changed from 'he desired' (a verb) to 'a desire' (a noun) by adding alif and hamza at the end
Changed from one desire to many desires by adding أ at the start - this is a common plural pattern in Arabic
Added ا after و to make the word into a broken plural form, showing multiple desires
Added هُم at the end to show these are their desires
Root letters ب-ع-د got vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make the common word 'after'

steps.m: after what (follows جَآءَكَ - came to you)
steps.n: Added مَا meaning 'what' and fatha before it to connect the words. This word connects to جَآءَكَ (came to you) in the ayah.
Added fatha vowels and the middle root letter ي changes to آ (long alif) to show past tense. This is a common pattern in Arabic verbs.
Added كَ at the end to show the action was done to 'you'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it shows the source or origin, connecting 'knowledge' to 'Allah'.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word starting with ال. This helps with pronunciation.
Transforms root into noun indicating field of knowledge/study
The ِ ending shows this word is connected to مِنَ (from) that comes before it in the ayah, meaning "from the knowledge"

steps.m: what/that which (connects to جَآءَكَ - came to you)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it links 'after' (بَعْدَ) with 'came to you' (جَآءَكَ), meaning 'after what/that which came to you'

steps.m: for you (connected to مِنَ ٱللَّهِ - from Allah)
steps.n: Added كَ (you) to لَ (for/to) to show who the action is for. This word connects to 'from Allah' in the ayah to mean 'for you from Allah'.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else. In this ayah, it's showing the knowledge (العِلْمِ) comes from Allah.
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word (العِلْمِ). This happens when مِن comes before words starting with ال.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, transforming it from 'a god' to 'The God' (Allah)
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This word is possessed by the word مِنَ (from) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'knowledge' (ٱلْعِلْمِ) to 'what/nothing' (ما) to express 'from the knowledge'

steps.m: to protect or be close to

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added shadda to change from verb to noun meaning 'one who does this action'
Added kasra tanween to show this is any protector (not a specific one) and comes after the word مِنَ (from)
Basic negative particle that means 'not'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb 'he protected' to a noun meaning 'one who protects' by adding alif - similar to how we add '-er' in English to make 'protect' into 'protector'
Changed ending to show this is 'any protector' rather than 'the protector'
Adds 'and' to connect this statement to what came before
Adds emphasis to show this is definitely true
قَدْ makes the certainty even stronger, like saying 'definitely' or 'certainly'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the first vowel to sukun makes the action causative

steps.m: We sent (subject of verb, taking رُسُلًا as its object)
steps.n: Adding نَا at the end shows that 'we' did the action. This word is followed by رُسُلًا (messengers) which receives the action of sending.
Added و between second and third letters to show someone who does the action regularly
Shortened the middle vowel and changed ending to make it plural (one messenger → many messengers)
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of sending in the phrase 'we sent messengers'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م in speech, causing the sounds to merge. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
The root letters form a noun meaning 'before'
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to form the basic noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'before you'
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The نَا at the end means 'we' - changes from 'he made' to 'we made'. The نَا is attached to the verb as its subject (doer of the action).
The وَ at the start means 'and' - adds conjunction meaning
Added fatha to the letter ل to show it's a preposition meaning 'to' or 'for'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is happening to 'them' (a group of people)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun for one spouse or mate
Added أَ at the beginning and ا after و to make it plural, showing multiple spouses or mates
